title: rurl: Parse, Clean, and Normalize URLs
description: A lightweight, vectorized toolkit for extracting structured information from URLs in R. Includes pipe-friendly functions for parsing, normalizing protocols, extracting domains, and constructing clean URLs. Domain and public-suffix extraction is delegated to the pslr package, which implements the Public Suffix List from publicsuffix.org. Punycode and IDNA encoding is handled by the punycoder package.
notes: Part of a three-package ecosystem for URL handling in R: rurl (URL parsing/normalization), pslr (Public Suffix List matching), and punycoder (Punycode/IDNA). The Public Suffix List data bundled in pslr is licensed under the Mozilla Public License 2.0, separately from the MIT-licensed package code.
references: Berners-Lee, T., Fielding, R., & Masinter, L. (2005). RFC 3986: Uniform Resource Identifier (URI): Generic Syntax. IETF.
Costello, A. (2003). RFC 3492: Punycode: A Bootstring encoding of Unicode for Internationalized Domain Names in Applications (IDNA). IETF.
Klensin, J. (2010). RFC 5890 & RFC 5891: Internationalized Domain Names for Applications (IDNA2008). IETF.
Unicode Consortium. UTS #46: Unicode IDNA Compatibility Processing. https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr46/
Mozilla Foundation and contributors. Public Suffix List. https://publicsuffix.org/
